attacking_style: Explosive attacks on brutal steep gradients (13%+), using sustained seated power to drop rivals. Prefers to attack decisively once rather than multiple accelerations, building gaps through raw power on severe pitches.
team_tactics: Insufficient data across multiple races to establish clear team tactical patterns.
terrain_preference: Excels on exceptionally steep gradient finishes (13% average), where raw power translates directly to gaps. Seated climbing style suggests preference for sustained steep pitches over variable terrain.
vulnerability: Insufficient multi-race data to identify clear weaknesses. Vulnerability on technical descents, longer sustained climbs, or against repeated accelerations remains unknown.
key_patterns: Devastating on 13%+ steep finishes, dropping all GC rivals through seated power.
Extends GC leads significantly in early race stages, establishing dominance before final week.
Tactical maturity belies Grand Tour leadership inexperience, combining strength with race intelligence.
form_trajectory: positive: Demonstrated Grand Tour GC capability with dominant performance on steep terrain, silencing doubters about leadership credentials in debut campaign
